Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and mild hyperchloremia with low total CO₂, which may indicate a non-anion gap metabolic acidosis. ALT is mildly elevated, possibly reflecting mild liver involvement, while AST is slightly low. Other parameters, including glucose, electrolytes, and lipids, are within normal limits. Clinical correlation and further evaluation of renal and liver function are recommended.
